message.content:
Research the MANAGEMENT, PROMOTERS, and ORDER BOOK of Vinyas Innovative Technologies Ltd, an Indian Electronics Manufacturing Services (EMS) company in Mysore (NSE: VINNY).

Use WebSearch then WebFetch on authoritative sources (vinyasit.com, annual reports, screener.in, moneycontrol, tofler, zaubacorp, news).

Gather:
1. Management & promoters: Managing Director / Chairman name, key executives, promoter background and promoter holding %.
2. Order book / order pipeline if disclosed (value, dates, growth).
3. Any recent large orders or contract wins announced.

You have WebSearch and WebFetch tools. Run several searches and fetch the most authoritative pages.

Return a concise markdown bullet list of findings. After EACH fact put the exact source URL in parentheses. Distinguish confirmed facts from estimates. Flag anything unverified. Do NOT write files — return findings as your text output.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Phases:
- Scope: Decompose question (from args) into 5 search angles
- Search: 5 parallel WebSearch agents, one per angle
- Fetch: URL-dedup, fetch top 15 sources, extract falsifiable claims
- Verify: 3-vote adversarial verification per claim (need 2/3 refutes to kill)
- Synthesize: Merge semantic dupes, rank by confidence, cite sources

As of March 31, 2026, Vinyas Innovative Technologies' order book stands at INR1,309 crores. This represents significant growth from earlier in the fiscal year, when the order book was INR1,062 crore as of September 30, 2025.

The execution period for the current order book is between 18 to 24 months, considering lead times for components and manufacturing.

## Recent Orders

Based on the search results, here are the key contract announcements from Vinyas Innovative Technologies:

## Recent Order Win (May 2026)

Vinyas Innovative Technologies has announced the receipt of significant purchase orders from both domestic and international clients, amounting to a total of ₹60.84 crore. The company secured a domestic order valued at ₹32.12 crore for the manufacture and supply of printed circuit board assemblies (PCBA), which is expected to be executed within a timeframe of 6 to 12 months. Additionally, Vinyas Innovative Technologies has been awarded an international contract valued at ₹28.72 crore for the manufacture and supply of PCBA with an execution period set between 12 to 18 months.

## Earlier Order Announcement (February 2025)

Vinyas Innovative Technologies Limited previously announced that it received multiple purchase orders worth ₹53.74 crore from various domestic and international customers for the manufacturing and supply of PCBA, to be executed over the next 24 months.

## Company Overview

Vinyas Innovative Technologies Limited, founded in 2001 and based in Mysore, is a premier Indian Electronics Manufacturing Services (EMS) provider specializing in end-to-end design, engineering, and manufacturing, serving high-complexity sectors like aerospace, defense, automotive, and medical.

input.prompt: List all named executives, directors, founder, managing director, chairman and their titles and backgrounds.
Report promoter holding percentage, latest shareholding pattern, names of promoters, key managerial personnel, and any order book figures mentioned.

content.content:
# Vinyas Innovative Technologies Ltd - Key Corporate Information

## Promoter Holding
**29.40%** as of March 2026 (latest shareholding pattern)

## Latest Shareholding Pattern (March 2026)
- Promoters: 29.40%
- FIIs: 0.49%
- DIIs: 6.53%
- Public: 63.59%
- Total Shareholders: 1,673

## Key Managerial Personnel
The webpage does not provide specific names of promoters or key managerial personnel. This information would require access to detailed board composition documents or annual reports not fully displayed on this page.

## Order Book
**₹1,309 Crore** (as of FY26, per the June 2026 earnings call update)

## Notable Context
The document notes that "Promoter holding is low: 29.4%" as a potential concern, and the company has shown significant revenue growth to ₹514 crore in FY26 with PAT of ₹30.87 crore. The company recently incorporated a wholly-owned subsidiary in the USA to support overseas expansion.

# Vinyas Innovative Technologies Order Win Details

**Total Contract Value:** ₹60.84 crore

**Domestic Order:**
- Value: ₹32.12 crore
- Product: Printed circuit board assemblies (PCBA)
- Execution Timeline: 6 to 12 months

**International Order:**
- Value: ₹28.72 crore
- Product: Printed circuit board assemblies (PCBA)
- Execution Timeline: 12 to 18 months

**Announcement Date:** May 15, 2026

**Additional Details:** According to the filing, "the company disclosed that it has secured a domestic order valued at ₹32.12 crore" for manufacturing and supply. Both contracts are described as part of the company's regular business operations with no related party involvement.

# Executive Leadership at Vinyas Innovative Technologies

**Narendra Narayanan** – Founder and Managing Director
- Engineering degree in Electrical & Electronics from University of Southern California
- Founded Vinyas in 2000
- Recipient of "The Best Employer" award (2011-2012) from India's Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment
- Member of Advisory Board, USC Viterbi School of Engineering

**Sumukh Narendra** – Director
- Bachelor's in Electronics and Communication Engineering from Sri Jayachamarajendra College of Engineering
- Master's in Embedded Systems from University of Pennsylvania
- Over 8 years in design engineering with experience at Fortune 100 companies
- Member of Confederation of Indian Industry (CII), Mysore Chapter

**T.R. Srinivasan** – Director - Operations
- Mechanical Engineer by qualification
- More than 20 years in electronics manufacturing and hardware industries
- Associated with Vinyas since 2001
- Oversees entire manufacturing facility

**Deepashree Narendra** – Vice President - Corporate Affairs
- Bachelor's in Biotechnology Engineering from Sri Jayachamarajendra College
- Postgraduate diploma in Intellectual Property Rights from National Law School, Bangalore
- Master's in Business Management (Communications) from Symbiosis School of Media and Communication
- Joined Vinyas in 2015; co-convener of CII Women Network Forum Mysuru

# Vinyas Innovative Technologies Ltd (NSE:VINYAS) - FY2026 Earnings Summary

## Order Book & Inflows
- **Order Book:** INR 1,309 crores as of March 31, 2026
- **Order Inflows FY26:** Approximately INR 960 crores
- **FY27 Guidance:** Expected inflows of "25% to 30% more than last year"

## Growth Guidance
For FY27, management projects EBITDA margins between 11% to 12%, down slightly from the FY26 margin of 12.5%. The company anticipates maintaining momentum beyond FY27.

## Execution Timeline
The current order book carries an "execution period between 18 to 24 months," accounting for component lead times and manufacturing schedules.

## Sector Breakdown
Defense dominates the portfolio, contributing approximately "85% to 90% of the order book." Other segments operate on rolling forecasts, with long-term orders primarily from defense and aerospace requirements.

## Key Management Commentary
Leadership emphasized strong fundamentals: the company achieved INR 518 crores in FY26 revenue with INR 30.87 crores in profit after tax. Export revenue represented approximately 50% of sales. However, capacity utilization remains modest at 35-40%, though management expects improvement as new capacity comes online within 2-3 months following INR 30 crores in recent capital expenditure.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Vinyas Innovative Technologies' defence joint venture in 2026:

## Joint Venture Overview

Vinyas Innovative Technologies has announced a strategic joint venture with an Israel-based defence technology company, aiming to expand its presence in the defence and aerospace sector in India.

## Key Terms and Structure

The collaboration is structured under a Joint Venture and Shareholders' Agreement, which outlines a 26:74 joint venture ratio. More specifically, Vinyas Innovative Technologies will hold a 26% stake, contributing capital equivalent to ₹2.6 crore.

## Operational Focus

The agreement focuses on manufacturing, assembly, system integration, maintenance, lifecycle support, and related activities within the defence and aerospace sector.

## Strategic Importance

This domestic joint venture is aligned with Vinyas Innovative Technologies' long-term strategic growth initiatives, enhancing its business and operational capabilities, and the company anticipates that this arrangement will bolster its strategic presence and business opportunities in the sector, supporting its long-term growth goals.

The arrangement remains subject to applicable statutory, regulatory and contractual approvals.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

# Vinyas Innovative Technologies Defence JV Details

**Partner:** Israel-based defence technology company

**Purpose:** "Strategic opportunities in the defence and aerospace sector" focusing on "manufacturing, assembly, system integration, maintenance, lifecycle support"

**Stake:** Vinyas holds 26% equity (₹2.6 crore investment); Israeli partner holds 74%

**Announcement Date:** May 26, 2026

**Key Details:** The joint venture operates as a domestic entity and is subject to regulatory approvals. This arrangement does not qualify as a related-party transaction.

content.text:
I have comprehensive, cross-verified findings from multiple authoritative sources. Here is the research report.

# Vinyas Innovative Technologies Ltd (NSE: VINYAS) — Management, Promoters & Order Book

*Note: The NSE ticker is VINYAS (the query's "VINNY" appears to be a typo). EMS company headquartered in Mysore (Mysuru), Karnataka, incorporated 2000/2001.*

## 1. Management & Promoters

- **Founder & Managing Director: Narendra Narayanan** — holds an engineering degree in Electrical & Electronics from University of Southern California; founded Vinyas in 2000; member of the Advisory Board, USC Viterbi School of Engineering. He is also described as Chairman/CEO. (https://www.vinyasit.com/executive-leadership/) (https://choiceindia.com/stocks/vinyas-innovative-technologies-ltd-board-of-directors)
- **Director: Sumukh Narendra** (son of the founder) — B.E. in Electronics & Communication (SJCE), Master's in Embedded Systems from University of Pennsylvania; with Vinyas since 2018; serves as Executive Director. (https://www.vinyasit.com/executive-leadership/) (https://in.linkedin.com/in/sumukh-n-2507bb17)
- **Director – Operations: T.R. Srinivasan** — Mechanical Engineer; 20+ years in electronics manufacturing; with Vinyas since 2001; oversees the manufacturing facility. (https://www.vinyasit.com/executive-leadership/)

## 2. Order Book / Pipeline (Confirmed via FY2026 earnings call, ~May/June 2026)

- **Order book: ₹1,309 crore as of March 31, 2026.** (https://ca.investing.com/news/company-news/vinyas-innovative-technologies-ltd-nsevinyas-full-year-2026-earnings-call-highlights-strong--4672642) (https://www.screener.in/company/VINYAS/)
- **Growth:** up from **₹1,062 crore as of September 30, 2025** — clear sequential growth through FY26. (https://ca.investing.com/news/company-news/vinyas-innovative-technologies-ltd-nsevinyas-q2-2026-earnings-call-highlights-strong--4305582)
- **Order inflows FY26: ~₹960 crore.** (https://ca.investing.com/news/company-news/vinyas-innovative-technologies-ltd-nsevinyas-full-year-2026-earnings-call-highlights-strong--4672642)
- **FY27 guidance:** management expects inflows "25%–30% more than last year"; order book gives visibility supporting 30%–35% growth; EBITDA margin guided to 11%–12% (vs 12.5% in FY26). (https://ca.investing.com/news/company-news/vinyas-innovative-technologies-ltd-nsevinyas-full-year-2026-earnings-call-highlights-strong--4672642)
- **Execution timeline:** 18–24 months for the current order book. (https://ca.investing.com/news/company-news/vinyas-innovative-technologies-ltd-nsevinyas-full-year-2026-earnings-call-highlights-strong--4672642)
- **Sector mix:** **Defence ~85%–90% of order book**; remainder aerospace, industrial, medical. (https://ca.investing.com/news/company-news/vinyas-innovative-technologies-ltd-nsevinyas-full-year-2026-earnings-call-highlights-strong--4672642)
- **Supporting financials (FY26):** Revenue ~₹514–518 crore; PAT ~₹30.87 crore; exports ~50% of sales; capacity utilization only ~35–40% (room to grow as ~₹30 crore capex comes online). (https://www.screener.in/company/VINYAS/) (https://ca.investing.com/news/company-news/vinyas-innovative-technologies-ltd-nsevinyas-full-year-2026-earnings-call-highlights-strong--4672642)

- **High confidence (multi-source / primary):** MD/founder identity, order book ₹1,309 cr (Mar 2026), promoter holding 29.40%, the ₹60.84 cr order, defence JV terms.
- **Medium / flag for verification:** the full named promoter list (single aggregator source); exact revenue (₹514 vs ₹518 cr — rounding across sources); older shareholding snapshots (29.39%, "58% insiders") which conflict with current Screener figures and appear dated.
- Aggregator sources (scanx, businessupturn, trendlyne) restate exchange filings; for legal certainty the underlying NSE/BSE corporate announcements and the FY26 annual report would be the primary documents to confirm.
